intro: Gerascanthus morelosanus, also known as "Morelos' shrub," is a plant species within the family Euphorbiaceae, characterized by its small, white flowers and dark green, elliptical leaves. Studies have shown that Gerascanthus morelosanus possesses various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to the treatment of conditions such as arthritis and certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Gerascanthus morelosanus is used in herbal medicine to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
